description: NOTE Applies only to the Aruba 6300 Switch Series. In a VSF stack, the switch may fail to log events for the matching access-list entries. ACL functionality is not impacted; access-list entries are applied properly and only the logging is incorrectly generated.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.15.1040, what caveat is documented for ACLs?

Feature Caveat: ACLs - NOTE Applies only to the Aruba 6300 Switch Series. In a VSF stack, the switch may fail to log events for the matching access-list entries. ACL functionality is not impacted; access-list entries are applied properly and only the logging is incorrectly generated.

description: If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.15.1040, what caveat is documented for Activate?

Feature Caveat: Activate - If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

description: The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.15.1040, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp.

description: Symptom: Users attempting to access the captive portal experience inconsistent behaviors such as: long delays (2-3 minutes) before the portal page loads, failure to display the portal page entirely, and certificate errors showing the switch’s local certificate. Scenario: When the captive portal service reaches its maximum session limits, the switch may fail to create new sessions, preventing new clients from being redirected properly.

description: Symptom: The Network Management Systems does not display IPv4 addresses for Aruba CX switches running AOS-CX version 10.13. Scenario: This issue occurs when the NMS queries the switch using SNMP and relies on either of the following OIDs to retrieve IPv4 address information: 1.3.6.1.2.1.4.20.1.2 (deprecated) 1.3.6.1.2.1.4.34.1.5 (current but not accessible in 10.13) In AOS-CX 10.13, neither OID returns valid IPv4 data, resulting in incomplete device visibility.

description: Symptom: In an L2VPN network, when a DHCP client sends an IP request with the broadcast flag unset (unicast), duplicate DHCP client packets are observed at the DHCP server from each VTEP. As a result, the client does not receive an IP address. Scenario:: To prevent duplicate DHCP client packets from each VTEP in an L2VPN topology, it is recommended to configure no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients on the switch. However, after saving the switch configuration and rebooting, the relay daemon does not correctly apply all configuration settings at boot time, even though the configuration persists in the database. This causes the duplicate packet issue to reappear. Workaround: To restore correct behavior, manually enable and then disable the L2VPN client configuration using dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients followed by no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients.

Workaround: To restore correct behavior, manually enable and then disable the L2VPN client configuration using dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients followed by no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients.
symptom: In an L2VPN network, when a DHCP client sends an IP request with the broadcast flag unset (unicast), duplicate DHCP client packets are observed at the DHCP server from each VTEP. As a result, the client does not receive an IP address.
scenario: To prevent duplicate DHCP client packets from each VTEP in an L2VPN topology, it is recommended to configure no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients on the switch. However, after saving the switch configuration and rebooting, the relay daemon does not correctly apply all configuration settings at boot time, even though the configuration persists in the database. This causes the duplicate packet issue to reappear.
workaround: To restore correct behavior, manually enable and then disable the L2VPN client configuration using dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients followed by no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.15.1040, what is the workaround for DHCP Relay Bug 371954?

DHCP Relay (Bug ID 371954): The documented workaround is: To restore correct behavior, manually enable and then disable the L2VPN client configuration using dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients followed by no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients.

description: Symptom: UBT client is not able to connect to UAC, causing it to remain in the Status: In Progress state. show ubt users all does not show the UBT tunnel as activated. Scenario:: This occurs when no enable is configured in one zone in a multi-zone configuration in the UBT switch. Workaround: Configure no enable in both zones, then configure enable.

description: Symptom: Port access client does not get a DHCP IP when there is a port-access reflexive policy assigned to its port-access role. Scenario: Port access client does not get a DHCP IP when there is a port-access reflexive policy assigned to its port-access role. This can cause traffic. Workaround: Dissociate the port-access policy from the role.

description: Symptom: NTP entries are not updating correctly with dhcpv4 and v6 client enable/disable Scenario 1: When DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 clients are on the same VLAN with DHCPv4 enabled and DHCPv6 disabled: Disabling DHCPv4 and enabling DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected Disabling both DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected When both DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 clients are on the same VLAN with DHCPv4 disabled and DHCPv6 enabled: Enabling DHCPv4 and disabling DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected Scenario 2: When DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 clients are on the different VLANs: Re-enabling DHCP mode on lower interface VLAN does not update the NTP entries as expected Disabling both DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected
symptom: NTP entries are not updating correctly with dhcpv4 and v6 client enable/disable
scenario: 2: When DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 clients are on the different VLANs: Re-enabling DHCP mode on lower interface VLAN does not update the NTP entries as expected Disabling both DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected

description: Symptom: A higher neighbor propagation delay will be observed when port speed is set to 2.5G. As a result, generalized Precision Time Protocol (gPTP) capability will become False and clients will not come up. Scenario: This issue can occur while changing port speeds to 2.5G on a 6300 switch using gPTP. Workaround: Use 1G/5G/10G port speeds instead.

description: Symptom: TFTP Software image upload/download transfer operation fails. Scenario: Downloading/uploading the software Image via sm ubuntu IPv6 TFTP server fails. Workaround: Use the blocksize option in the copy command with a blocksize of 1375 or less. For example: Copy copy tftp://[20:1::100];blocksize=1375/image.swi secondary vrf vrf1
symptom: TFTP Software image upload/download transfer operation fails.

description: Symptom: Traffic is dropped for a few seconds on a MACsec channel during a VSF switchover. Scenario: When the MACsec channel has data-delay protection enabled, there can be traffic drops for a few seconds on the channel post a VSF switchover due the reset of the MKA session on the interface. Workaround: Do not use data-delay protection in a MACsec policy if the system is deployed as a VSF stack.
symptom: Traffic is dropped for a few seconds on a MACsec channel during a VSF switchover.

workaround: Do not use data-delay protection in a MACsec policy if the system is deployed as a VSF stack.
